title: Using Nagios for small business infrastructure monitoring
excerpt: There are a million tools out there for monitoring services and measuring application performance, and I’m sure many of them are awesome. When I went looking for a good service and infrastructure monitoring solution for our community newspaper’s one-person IT department (me), I came back to an open source tool that I’d probably first started … Continue reading Using Nagios for small business infrastructure monitoring
content_html:
There are a million tools out there for monitoring services and measuring application performance, and I’m sure many of them are awesome.

When I went looking for a good service and infrastructure monitoring solution for our community newspaper’s one-person IT department (me), I came back to an open source tool that I’d probably first started using over 20 years ago: Nagios.

It works great for us and only costs $4/month to self-host. I thought I’d share about how we’re using it in case others might find it helpful.

My needs were pretty straightforward.

I wanted to monitor 10 or so servers and network devices and various services hosted on them, the health of 20 or so web applications, and the status pages of about 20 external services/platforms we depend on.

When something gets slow or stops responding, I wanted to know about it in a reliable way.

We’d used Nagios extensively at the website development and hosting business I ran a long time ago, and it was great for this kind of thing. Highly configurable and flexible, low maintenance, and very reliable.

When looking at options more recently, Nagios didn’t seem to have changed much in all that time, and with its outdated web interface and arcane config file syntax, I convinced myself that someone had surely created a better tool by now. So, I spent a bunch of time researching alternatives, including Zabbix, Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, New Relic, Pingdom, UptimeRobot, and a bunch of others. And we did start out using UptimeRobot for a brief while.

But when I looked at the amount of time I was going to spend learning and managing the complexity of those stacks, or the amount of money we would spend on SaaS tools along with some of the constraints and “per seat” or “per monitor” limits they imposed, the more I was ready to go with a tried and true, purpose-built option that could scale up without additional cost or complexity.

Nagios is not fun to set up initially — see my earlier comment about arcane config syntax. It probably took me the equivalent of a full day to get everything just so. But once I did, it’s been humming along great, and brings significant peace of mind.

We’re currently monitoring 54 hosts and 104 services. This is all on a $4/month droplet at Digital Ocean, 1 vCPU, 512 MB RAM, 10 GB Disk. We pay another $0.80/month for weekly droplet snapshots. We set our monitoring droplet up in a different physical region from the rest of our servers/droplets, on the other side of the continent.

To get going, I started with these instructions from Edson Kimuli, which were very helpful. A few things I did during/after:

  • Used a more modern PHP version
  • Removed apache2
  • Set up certbot for SSL usage
  • Added a swap partition
  • Installed and configured postfix for email sending, although I don’t think we’re really using it
  • Installed Perl for using some Nagios plugins
  • Locked down network access to the monitoring host

What we monitor

Here are some of the things we’re monitoring:

  • That a given host is up via ping
  • Web server is available
  • Web server responds to a given request via Server Name Indication (SNI)
  • SSL certificate for web server is valid and not about to expire
  • DNS servers respond with the expected IP addresses for given hostnames
  • Domain expiration is not imminent for critical domains
  • Droplet disk space, load, and memory usage is as expected
  • Server backups have successfully run recently
  • SSH services are available and listening
  • Web applications provide an expected response at a health endpoint
  • Squid proxy server is running and accepting requests
  • Proxy server serves up a PAC file with the expected contents
  • Proxy server actually proxies requests, but doesn’t allow unauthenticated proxying
  • Critical websites serve expected content at key pages, including their RSS feeds
  • That our office networks are reachable from the public Internet
  • VPN servers are accepting connections

For external status page monitoring, we basically check to make sure an “All Systems Operational” message is displayed according to whatever status page provider the SaaS tool we care about is using. This is a bit broad since many status pages display maintenance activities as an interruption to that all green status, but it works for now.

One thing I really like about Nagios is that if it doesn’t have a built in monitor for something, there’s probably a plugin for it, or I can build one myself with a simple shell script. I don’t have to install heavy third-party applications on every server just to have them participate in monitoring.

One random example is that Google’s status page doesn’t display a simple way to tell if all services are up and running — the “no incidents” indicator is a bit separate, so I wrote a simple if slightly brittle script to check how many of its services are listed as available:

#!/bin/bash

# Checks that the appropriate number of Google Workspace services are listed as available.

# URL to check
url="https://www.google.com/appsstatus/dashboard/"

# Run the command and store the output in a variable
output=$(curl -sSf "$url" | grep -oh 'psd__available'  | wc -l)

# Check if the output is equal to 33
if [ "$output" -eq 33 ]; then
    echo "OK - Expected output received: $output from $url"
    exit 0  # Nagios status code for OK
else
    echo "CRITICAL - Unexpected output received: $output from $url"
    exit 2  # Nagios status code for Critical
fi

Then in Nagios, you can set that script up as a “check command” that can be used to determine the health/status of a host you are monitoring:

define command {
    command_name    check-google-workspace
    command_line    /usr/local/nagios/plugins/check_google_workspace.sh
}

Even though this is a bit weird and requires occasional changes when Google adds or removes services, it’s one of those things that I think would be harder to do than I wanted with some SaaS tools.

We do also use the Nagios NRPE agent for a lightweight service check harness that allows me to then set up custom checks on a given server. If you use it, make sure you set up a firewall rule to restrict access from only your Nagios monitor.

Alerting

As an IT department of one, I need to make sure I don’t miss alerts, but I also don’t want to be overwhelmed by them.

So for now, I send all alerts to a dedicated Slack channel and important alerts to my mobile device using Pushover (another classic service that has been around forever and just works). Here’s the Pushover notification script we use. Alert payloads can be customized so that they do or don’t break through my “do not disturb” and sleep settings.

I also use the easyNag iOS app to be able to quickly review and act on alerts. Although I can get to the Nagios web interface quickly, I’ve found having a native app to quickly acknowledge an outage or review host/service status gives me the fast and modern ops feel I want when there’s too much to do.

Monitoring the monitor

What if Nagios goes down? It hasn’t happened for this installation yet, but just in case, I have a basic meta monitor in place using a free plan at Healthchecks.io. Nagios “checks” a Healthchecks.io endpoint every 5 minutes and if it hasn’t send a request within the expected window, Healthchecks.io will alert me via email, Slack and Pushover.

The relief of having good monitoring

To perhaps state the obvious, I’ve found that monitoring the status of all the systems and services we depend on brings reassurance and faster resolutions.

If there are mumblings spreading among my team about an outage, I usually already know about it and pinpointing the cause.

When one of our office location ISPs experiences a fiber cut, I’m usually the one to tell them about it, after convincing them that rebooting our router is not going to help.

And when I’m making configuration changes or upgrading something, it’s nice to know that many kinds of critical errors would be caught quickly.

If you aren’t monitoring your small business infrastructure, I highly recommend it.

blocks.html_en: For external status page monitoring, we basically check to make sure an “All Systems Operational” message is displayed according to whatever status page provider the SaaS tool we care about is using. This is a bit broad since many status pages display maintenance activities as an interruption to that all green status, but it works for now.

sentences.en: For external status page monitoring, we basically check to make sure an “All Systems Operational” message is displayed according to whatever status page provider the SaaS tool we care about is using.
sentences.zh: 对于外部状态页面监控，我们基本上会检查以确保根据我们关心的 SaaS 工具正在使用的任何状态页面提供商显示“所有系统运行”消息。

sentences.en: This is a bit broad since many status pages display maintenance activities as an interruption to that all green status, but it works for now.
sentences.zh: 这有点宽泛，因为许多状态页面将维护活动显示为对所有绿色状态的中断，但它目前有效。

blocks.html_en: One thing I really like about Nagios is that if it doesn’t have a built in monitor for something, there’s probably a plugin for it, or I can build one myself with a simple shell script. I don’t have to install heavy third-party applications on every server just to have them participate in monitoring.

sentences.en: One thing I really like about Nagios is that if it doesn’t have a built in monitor for something, there’s probably a plugin for it, or I can build one myself with a simple shell script.
sentences.zh: 我真正喜欢 Nagios 的一件事是，如果它没有内置的监视器，可能有一个插件，或者我可以用一个简单的 shell 脚本自己构建一个。

sentences.en: I don’t have to install heavy third-party applications on every server just to have them participate in monitoring.
sentences.zh: 我不必在每台服务器上安装繁重的第三方应用程序只是为了让它们参与监控。

blocks.html_en: One random example is that Google’s status page doesn’t display a simple way to tell if all services are up and running — the “no incidents” indicator is a bit separate, so I wrote a simple if slightly brittle script to check how many of its services are listed as available:

sentences.en: One random example is that Google’s status page doesn’t display a simple way to tell if all services are up and running — the “no incidents” indicator is a bit separate, so I wrote a simple if slightly brittle script to check how many of its services are listed as available:
sentences.zh: 一个随机的例子是，Google 的状态页面没有显示一种简单的方式来判断所有服务是否已启动并正在运行 - “无事件”指示器有点独立，因此我编写了一个简单但稍显脆弱的脚本来检查其有多少服务被列为可用：

blocks.html_en: Then in Nagios, you can set that script up as a “check command” that can be used to determine the health/status of a host you are monitoring:

sentences.en: Then in Nagios, you can set that script up as a “check command” that can be used to determine the health/status of a host you are monitoring:
sentences.zh: 然后在 Nagios 中，您可以将该脚本设置为“检查命令”，可用于确定您正在监视的主机的运行状况/状态：

blocks.html_en: Even though this is a bit weird and requires occasional changes when Google adds or removes services, it’s one of those things that I think would be harder to do than I wanted with some SaaS tools.

sentences.en: Even though this is a bit weird and requires occasional changes when Google adds or removes services, it’s one of those things that I think would be harder to do than I wanted with some SaaS tools.
sentences.zh: 尽管这有点奇怪，并且在 Google 添加或删除服务时需要偶尔进行更改，但我认为使用某些 SaaS 工具比我想要的更难做到这一点。

blocks.html_en: We do also use the Nagios NRPE agent for a lightweight service check harness that allows me to then set up custom checks on a given server. If you use it, make sure you set up a firewall rule to restrict access from only your Nagios monitor.

sentences.en: We do also use the Nagios NRPE agent for a lightweight service check harness that allows me to then set up custom checks on a given server.
sentences.zh: 我们还使用 Nagios NRPE 代理作为轻量级服务检查工具，使我能够在给定服务器上设置自定义检查。

sentences.en: If you use it, make sure you set up a firewall rule to restrict access from only your Nagios monitor.
sentences.zh: 如果您使用它，请确保设置防火墙规则以限制仅来自 Nagios 监视器的访问。

blocks.html_en: Alerting

sentences.en: Alerting
sentences.zh: 警报

blocks.html_en: As an IT department of one, I need to make sure I don’t miss alerts, but I also don’t want to be overwhelmed by them.

sentences.en: As an IT department of one, I need to make sure I don’t miss alerts, but I also don’t want to be overwhelmed by them.
sentences.zh: 作为 IT 部门的一员，我需要确保我不会错过警报，但我也不想被警报淹没。

blocks.html_en: So for now, I send all alerts to a dedicated Slack channel and important alerts to my mobile device using Pushover (another classic service that has been around forever and just works). Here’s the Pushover notification script we use. Alert payloads can be customized so that they do or don’t break through my “do not disturb” and sleep settings.

sentences.en: So for now, I send all alerts to a dedicated Slack channel and important alerts to my mobile device using Pushover (another classic service that has been around forever and just works).
sentences.zh: 因此，目前，我使用 Pushover（另一个一直存在且有效的经典服务）将所有警报发送到专用的 Slack 通道，并将重要警报发送到我的移动设备。

sentences.en: Here’s the Pushover notification script we use.
sentences.zh: 这是我们使用的 Pushover 通知脚本。

sentences.en: Alert payloads can be customized so that they do or don’t break through my “do not disturb” and sleep settings.
sentences.zh: 警报有效负载可以自定义，以便它们可以或不会突破我的“请勿打扰”和睡眠设置。

blocks.html_en: I also use the easyNag iOS app to be able to quickly review and act on alerts. Although I can get to the Nagios web interface quickly, I’ve found having a native app to quickly acknowledge an outage or review host/service status gives me the fast and modern ops feel I want when there’s too much to do.

sentences.en: I also use the easyNag iOS app to be able to quickly review and act on alerts.
sentences.zh: 我还使用 easyNag iOS 应用程序来快速查看警报并采取行动。

sentences.en: Although I can get to the Nagios web interface quickly, I’ve found having a native app to quickly acknowledge an outage or review host/service status gives me the fast and modern ops feel I want when there’s too much to do.
sentences.zh: 虽然我可以快速访问 Nagios Web 界面，但我发现拥有一个本地应用程序来快速确认中断或查看主机/服务状态可以让我在有太多事情要做时获得我想要的快速和现代的操作感觉。

blocks.html_en: Monitoring the monitor

sentences.en: Monitoring the monitor
sentences.zh: 监控监视器

blocks.html_en: What if Nagios goes down? It hasn’t happened for this installation yet, but just in case, I have a basic meta monitor in place using a free plan at Healthchecks.io. Nagios “checks” a Healthchecks.io endpoint every 5 minutes and if it hasn’t send a request within the expected window, Healthchecks.io will alert me via email, Slack and Pushover.

sentences.en: What if Nagios goes down?
sentences.zh: 如果 Nagios 宕机了怎么办？

sentences.en: It hasn’t happened for this installation yet, but just in case, I have a basic meta monitor in place using a free plan at Healthchecks.io.
sentences.zh: 这次安装还没有发生这种情况，但为了以防万一，我使用 Healthchecks.io 上的免费计划安装了一个基本的元监视器。

sentences.en: Nagios “checks” a Healthchecks.io endpoint every 5 minutes and if it hasn’t send a request within the expected window, Healthchecks.io will alert me via email, Slack and Pushover.
sentences.zh: Nagios 每 5 分钟“检查”一次 Healthchecks.io 端点，如果它没有在预期窗口内发送请求，Healthchecks.io 将通过电子邮件、Slack 和 Pushover 提醒我。

blocks.html_en: The relief of having good monitoring

sentences.en: The relief of having good monitoring
sentences.zh: 良好监控带来的缓解

blocks.html_en: To perhaps state the obvious, I’ve found that monitoring the status of all the systems and services we depend on brings reassurance and faster resolutions.

sentences.en: To perhaps state the obvious, I’ve found that monitoring the status of all the systems and services we depend on brings reassurance and faster resolutions.
sentences.zh: 也许显而易见的是，我发现监控我们所依赖的所有系统和服务的状态可以带来安心和更快的解决方案。

blocks.html_en: If there are mumblings spreading among my team about an outage, I usually already know about it and pinpointing the cause.

sentences.en: If there are mumblings spreading among my team about an outage, I usually already know about it and pinpointing the cause.
sentences.zh: 如果我的团队中有关于停电的抱怨，我通常已经知道并查明原因。

blocks.html_en: When one of our office location ISPs experiences a fiber cut, I’m usually the one to tell them about it, after convincing them that rebooting our router is not going to help.

sentences.en: When one of our office location ISPs experiences a fiber cut, I’m usually the one to tell them about it, after convincing them that rebooting our router is not going to help.
sentences.zh: 当我们的办公地点之一的 ISP 遇到光纤中断时，我通常会在说服他们重新启动路由器不会有帮助之后告诉他们这一情况。

blocks.html_en: And when I’m making configuration changes or upgrading something, it’s nice to know that many kinds of critical errors would be caught quickly.

sentences.en: And when I’m making configuration changes or upgrading something, it’s nice to know that many kinds of critical errors would be caught quickly.
sentences.zh: 当我进行配置更改或升级某些内容时，很高兴知道许多类型的严重错误都会被快速捕获。

blocks.html_en: If you aren’t monitoring your small business infrastructure, I highly recommend it.

sentences.en: If you aren’t monitoring your small business infrastructure, I highly recommend it.
sentences.zh: 如果您不监控小型企业基础设施，我强烈推荐它。

content_html_zh:
有上百万种工具可用于监控服务和测量应用程序性能，我相信其中许多工具都很棒。

当我为社区报纸的单人 IT 部门（我）寻找良好的服务和基础设施监控解决方案时，我回到了可能在 20 多年前就开始使用的开源工具：Nagios。

它对我们来说非常有用，并且每月只需花费 4 美元即可自行托管。 我想我应该分享一下我们如何使用它，以防其他人发现它有帮助。

我的需求非常简单。

我想要监控 10 个左右的服务器和网络设备以及它们上托管的各种服务、20 个左右的 Web 应用程序的运行状况以及我们依赖的大约 20 个外部服务/平台的状态页面。

当某些事情变得缓慢或停止响应时，我想以可靠的方式了解它。

很久以前，我们在我经营的网站开发和托管业务中广泛使用了 Nagios，它对于此类事情非常有用。 高度可配置、灵活、维护成本低且非常可靠。

最近查看选项时，Nagios 似乎一直以来都没有发生太大变化，并且凭借其过时的 Web 界面和神秘的配置文件语法，我说服自己现在肯定有人创建了更好的工具。 因此，我花了很多时间研究替代方案，包括 Zabbix、Prometheus、Grafana、Datadog、New Relic、Pingdom、UptimeRobot 等。 我们确实开始使用 UptimeRobot 一段时间。

但是，当我考虑到我要花在学习和管理这些堆栈的复杂性上的时间，或者我们在 SaaS 工具上花费的金额以及它们施加的一些约束和“每个席位”或“每个显示器”限制时，我就越准备好使用一个经过考验的、真实的、专门构建的选项，该选项可以在不增加成本或复杂性的情况下进行扩展。

Nagios 最初设置起来并不有趣——请参阅我之前关于神秘配置语法的评论。 我可能花了一整天的时间才把一切都搞定。 但一旦我这样做了，它就一直在嗡嗡作响，并带来极大的内心平静。

我们目前正在监控 54 个主机和 104 个服务。 这一切都在 Digital Ocean 每月 4 美元的 Droplet 上实现，配备 1 个 vCPU、512 MB RAM、10 GB 磁盘。 我们每月另外支付 0.80 美元以获得每周的液滴快照。 我们将监控 Droplet 设置在与大陆另一边的其他服务器/Droplet 不同的物理区域。

首先，我从埃德森·基穆利 (Edson Kimuli) 的这些指示开始，这些指示非常有帮助。 在此期间/之后我做了一些事情：

  • 使用更现代的 PHP 版本
  • 删除了阿帕奇2
  • 设置 certbot 以使用 SSL
  • 添加了交换分区
  • 安装并配置了用于电子邮件发送的 postfix，尽管我认为我们并没有真正使用它
  • 安装 Perl 以使用一些 Nagios 插件
  • 锁定对监控主机的网络访问

我们监控什么

以下是我们正在监控的一些内容：

  • 通过 ping 确定给定主机已启动
  • 网络服务器可用
  • Web 服务器通过服务器名称指示 (SNI) 响应给定请求
  • Web 服务器的 SSL 证书有效且不会过期
  • DNS 服务器使用给定主机名的预期 IP 地址进行响应
  • 关键域名不会很快到期
  • Droplet 磁盘空间、负载和内存使用情况符合预期
  • 服务器备份最近已成功运行
  • SSH 服务可用并正在监听
  • Web 应用程序在健康端点提供预期响应
  • Squid 代理服务器正在运行并接受请求
  • 代理服务器提供包含预期内容的 PAC 文件
  • 代理服务器实际上代理请求，但不允许未经身份验证的代理
  • 关键网站在关键页面上提供预期内容，包括其 RSS 源
  • 我们的办公网络可以通过公共互联网访问
  • VPN 服务器正在接受连接

对于外部状态页面监控，我们基本上会检查以确保根据我们关心的 SaaS 工具正在使用的任何状态页面提供商显示“所有系统运行”消息。 这有点宽泛，因为许多状态页面将维护活动显示为对所有绿色状态的中断，但它目前有效。

我真正喜欢 Nagios 的一件事是，如果它没有内置的监视器，可能有一个插件，或者我可以用一个简单的 shell 脚本自己构建一个。 我不必在每台服务器上安装繁重的第三方应用程序只是为了让它们参与监控。

一个随机的例子是，Google 的状态页面没有显示一种简单的方式来判断所有服务是否已启动并正在运行 - “无事件”指示器有点独立，因此我编写了一个简单但稍显脆弱的脚本来检查其有多少服务被列为可用：

#!/bin/bash

# Checks that the appropriate number of Google Workspace services are listed as available.

# URL to check
url="https://www.google.com/appsstatus/dashboard/"

# Run the command and store the output in a variable
output=$(curl -sSf "$url" | grep -oh 'psd__available'  | wc -l)

# Check if the output is equal to 33
if [ "$output" -eq 33 ]; then
    echo "OK - Expected output received: $output from $url"
    exit 0  # Nagios status code for OK
else
    echo "CRITICAL - Unexpected output received: $output from $url"
    exit 2  # Nagios status code for Critical
fi

然后在 Nagios 中，您可以将该脚本设置为“检查命令”，可用于确定您正在监视的主机的运行状况/状态：

define command {
    command_name    check-google-workspace
    command_line    /usr/local/nagios/plugins/check_google_workspace.sh
}

尽管这有点奇怪，并且在 Google 添加或删除服务时需要偶尔进行更改，但我认为使用某些 SaaS 工具比我想要的更难做到这一点。

我们还使用 Nagios NRPE 代理作为轻量级服务检查工具，使我能够在给定服务器上设置自定义检查。 如果您使用它，请确保设置防火墙规则以限制仅来自 Nagios 监视器的访问。

警报

作为 IT 部门的一员，我需要确保我不会错过警报，但我也不想被警报淹没。

因此，目前，我使用 Pushover（另一个一直存在且有效的经典服务）将所有警报发送到专用的 Slack 通道，并将重要警报发送到我的移动设备。 这是我们使用的 Pushover 通知脚本。 警报有效负载可以自定义，以便它们可以或不会突破我的“请勿打扰”和睡眠设置。

我还使用 easyNag iOS 应用程序来快速查看警报并采取行动。 虽然我可以快速访问 Nagios Web 界面，但我发现拥有一个本地应用程序来快速确认中断或查看主机/服务状态可以让我在有太多事情要做时获得我想要的快速和现代的操作感觉。

监控监视器

如果 Nagios 宕机了怎么办？ 这次安装还没有发生这种情况，但为了以防万一，我使用 Healthchecks.io 上的免费计划安装了一个基本的元监视器。 Nagios 每 5 分钟“检查”一次 Healthchecks.io 端点，如果它没有在预期窗口内发送请求，Healthchecks.io 将通过电子邮件、Slack 和 Pushover 提醒我。

良好监控带来的缓解

也许显而易见的是，我发现监控我们所依赖的所有系统和服务的状态可以带来安心和更快的解决方案。

如果我的团队中有关于停电的抱怨，我通常已经知道并查明原因。

当我们的办公地点之一的 ISP 遇到光纤中断时，我通常会在说服他们重新启动路由器不会有帮助之后告诉他们这一情况。

当我进行配置更改或升级某些内容时，很高兴知道许多类型的严重错误都会被快速捕获。

如果您不监控小型企业基础设施，我强烈推荐它。
